title: Systems and methods for forming nitrogen-based compounds
abstract: A system for forming ammonia including an anode, a cathode in electrical communication with the anode, and a catalyst material positioned in an electrical communication pathway between the cathode and the anode, the catalyst material including nanoparticles including at least one of a conductor and a semiconductor, the nanoparticles having an interior cavity, wherein the system is configured to use nitrogen and water to generate ammonia.
claims: 1. A method comprising: synthesizing a nitrogen-based compound from nitrogen via an electrochemical nitrogen reduction reaction (NRR) in the presence of a catalyst material comprising nanoparticles; wherein: a yield of the nitrogen-based compound is at least 0.87 μg cm −2 h −1 ; a Faradaic efficiency of the nitrogen-based compound is at least 8.2%; and at least one of: the nanoparticles comprise hollow gold nanocages (AuHNCs); or at least a portion of the nanoparticles has an interior cavity with a cross-sectional dimension from 5 nm to 50 nm.
